iocs.incident: March 2025 supply chain compromise; malicious commit replaced multiple tagged versions to dump secrets via base64 in workflow logs. The upstream repository carries 379 historical tags spanning v1 through v47; the GHSA-mrrh-fwg8-r2c3 advisory names only the major-version floating tags (v35-v46) and three specific retroactively-updated references (v1.0.0, v35.7.7-sec, v44.5.1) as definitively compromised. Per-point-release enumeration is intentionally omitted: the malicious commit was reachable via the major-version floating tags during the exposure window, and any pinned point release within v1-v45.0.7 should be re-verified against its commit SHA rather than the tag name. Coverage is capped at the 12 floating-major tags because exhaustive enumeration of the 379-tag history exceeds the corpus design budget (per-entry cap of 200) without adding detection value.

March 2025 supply chain compromise; only the floating tag v1 was force-pushed to a malicious commit that exfiltrated runner secrets via workflow logs between 18:42 and 20:31 UTC on 2025-03-11. The 21 pinned point-release tags upstream (v1.0, v1.0.0 through v1.5.0) were not retagged and remain at their original SHAs; per GHSA-qmg3-hpqr-gqvc, v1 is the sole compromised reference. Downstream actions reviewdog/action-shellcheck, action-staticcheck, action-composite-template, action-ast-grep, and action-typos all inherit the compromise via their dependency on action-setup@v1.

Reported as part of broader 2025 GitHub Actions supply-chain disclosures; treat tag v1 as suspect until pinned to a vetted SHA. No primary-source advisory has been located, so this entry should be re-evaluated once one surfaces.

March 2026 TeamPCP supply chain compromise; attacker force-pushed 76 of 77 version tags (range v0.0.1 through v0.34.2) to malicious commits that injected a credential stealer into entrypoint.sh. Exposure window for trivy-action ran from ~17:43 UTC 2026-03-19 to ~05:40 UTC 2026-03-20. Tag v0.35.0 was the sole untouched tag and was the first protected by GitHub's immutable-releases feature; v0.36.0 is a post-remediation safe release. 75 of the 76 compromised tags are enumerated below; the 76th could not be identified from primary sources (upstream restored 73 of the 76 to clean commits; v0.0.10, v0.34.1, and v0.34.2 remain unrestored at the time of writing). All listed tags must be treated as malicious regardless of current upstream state.

March 2026 TeamPCP supply chain compromise; all 7 existing tags (v0.2.0 through v0.2.6) were force-pushed to malicious commits that injected a credential stealer as a 'Setup environment' step. Exposure window ran from ~17:43 UTC to ~21:44 UTC on 2026-03-19. Companion compromise to aquasecurity/trivy-action.

March 2026 TeamPCP supply chain compromise; all 35 Git tags enumerated below were redirected to malicious commits that injected a credential stealer into setup.sh, performing CI/CD runner memory dumps and exfiltrating to checkmarx.zone. Attack window ran from 12:58 to 16:50 UTC on 2026-03-23. The cx-plugins-releases service account (GitHub ID 225848595) was the publishing identity. SHAs listed are the leading examples published by the Wiz advisory.

March 2026 TeamPCP supply chain compromise; only tag 2.3.28 has been directly observed as malicious, but TeamPCP's broader pattern (Trivy, KICS) was to retag the full set. Treat any non-pinned reference as suspect until Checkmarx publishes a complete tag inventory.
